round1: Round 1 is wrapped, and while everyone correctly picked Anaheim to advance, the rest of your brackets look like a toddler scribbled on them. Stephanie, your picks were so off, I'm starting to think you were betting against yourself, and for those who backed Atlanta, congratulations on picking the quickest exit.
round2: Alright, Round Two is in the books. Looks like most of you forgot the Ducks existed, with everyone picking Vancouver to win. Jaclyn and Dad had some moments of clarity, but Mom's picks are now officially in the witness protection program.
round3: Round 3 is in the books, and while Marc and Dad correctly predicted the Ducks would take it in six, a few of you apparently missed the memo that Buffalo wasn't going to the dance. The Senators made quick work of the Sabres in five, leaving a couple of picks looking about as useful as a goalie with a broken stick.
round4: Round 4 is done, and the Anaheim Ducks are your 2007 Stanley Cup Champions, winning in 5 games. Every single participant picked Ottawa to win and Anaheim to take precisely zero games, proving that collective delusion is alive and well in this pool. Congratulations on whiffing so spectacularly.
overall: Alright, another playoff season is in the books, and what a spectacular display of collective ineptitude we just witnessed in the 2007 NHL playoff pool! For the first time ever, every single participant – Marc, Derrick, Chrissy, Jaclyn, and Stephanie – managed to finish with a grand total of zero points, making them all joint champions of absolute futility. Marc, a five-time winner, truly "dominated" the field by tying for first with a perfect score of zero, proving that even seasoned champions can completely whiff on their picks. Chrissy, you deserve a special mention for somehow finishing dead last with zero points, which is a rare feat when everyone else is equally clueless. Let's just say the only thing more shocking than the Ducks winning the Cup was the collective embarrassment of this entire pool.
